message:
fix(packs/core/git): catch combined branch force-delete flags

The branch-force-delete rule only matched `-D`, `-f`, or `--force`
verbatim. Combined short-flag forms that are functionally identical to
`-D` — `-Dr` (force-delete remote-tracking), `-vD` (verbose + force
delete), `-fv`, `-vdf` — slipped past because `-[Dfv]` etc. weren't
parsed as a single multi-letter short-flag.

Fixed by expanding the flag match to `-[a-zA-Z]*[Df][a-zA-Z]*\b` and
requiring preceding whitespace via the existing `(?:\S+\s+)*` token
walker — so hyphens INSIDE branch names (`merged-feature`,
`security-fix`) don't false-match as flags. Lowercase `-d` alone
(merged-only, non-forcing delete) remains allowed.

Regression tests added for every combined form plus negative cases.
